commands.name: openphone.device.status
commands.description: Gather a call context card from call log, contacts, messages, and calendar, including missed calls not yet returned.

query.description: Optional person, business, trip, or confirmation query.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows per source.

commands.name: openphone.apps.search
commands.description: Search launchable installed Android apps by label, package, or activity.

query.description: Optional app label, package, or activity query.

limit.description: Maximum number of apps to return.

reason.description: Why installed app context is needed.

commands.name: openphone.screen.get
commands.description: Read the current phone screen and optional screenshot.

commands.name: openphone.screen.understand_local
commands.description: Run bounded local screen understanding on the phone without returning screenshot bytes.

max_visible_text.description: Maximum visible text entries to return, capped by the phone.

max_interactive_elements.description: Maximum interactive elements to return, capped by the phone.

timeout_ms.description: Requested compute timeout in milliseconds, capped by the phone.

reason.description: Why local screen understanding is needed.

commands.name: openphone.notifications.list
commands.description: List recent indexed Android notifications.

limit.description: Maximum number of notifications to return.

commands.name: openphone.notifications.search
commands.description: Search recent indexed Android notifications.

limit.description: Maximum number of notifications to return.

commands.name: openphone.notifications.open
commands.description: Open an active Android notification through its content intent.

commands.name: openphone.contacts.search
commands.description: Search Android contacts by name, phone, email, or organization.

query.description: Optional name, phone, or email query.

limit.description: Maximum number of contacts to return.

commands.name: openphone.calendar.search
commands.description: Search Android calendar events in a bounded time window.

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return.

commands.name: openphone.calendar.add
commands.description: Create an Android calendar event in the user's writable calendar.

all_day.description: Whether this is an all-day event.

reason.description: Why this event should be created.

commands.name: openphone.calendar.update
commands.description: Update fields of an existing Android calendar event (title, time, location, description). Recurring events update the whole series.

start_at.description: New start, Unix time in milliseconds.

end_at.description: New end, Unix time in milliseconds.

duration_minutes.description: New duration if end_at is omitted.

all_day.description: Whether this is an all-day event.

reason.description: Why this event should be changed.

commands.name: openphone.calendar.delete
commands.description: Delete an Android calendar event permanently. Recurring events delete the whole series.

reason.description: Why this event should be deleted.

commands.name: openphone.messages.search
commands.description: Search Android SMS message history by sender, body, or address.

query.description: Optional sender, body, or address query.

limit.description: Maximum number of messages to return.

commands.name: openphone.messages.draft
commands.description: Prepare a structured SMS draft for review without sending it.

contact_query.description: Contact name if recipient should be resolved.

commands.name: openphone.messages.send
commands.description: Send an SMS message to a phone number.

reason.description: Why this SMS should be sent.

commands.name: openphone.calls.search
commands.description: Search Android call log entries by number, contact name (joined from contacts when the log has none), type, or date range.

query.description: Optional number or cached contact name.

type.description: Optional call type filter: missed, incoming, outgoing, voicemail, rejected, or blocked.

limit.description: Maximum number of calls to return.

since.description: Only calls at or after this Unix time in milliseconds, or 0.

until.description: Only calls at or before this Unix time in milliseconds, or 0.

reason.description: Why call history context is needed.

commands.name: openphone.calls.place
commands.description: Place a phone call to a number.

contact_query.description: Contact name if number should be resolved.

reason.description: Why this call should be placed.

commands.name: openphone.memory.search
commands.description: Search durable OpenPhone memories: user preferences, facts, standing instructions, and long-lived personal context. Use before answering questions that depend on remembered user preferences or identity.

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return.

commands.name: openphone.memory.save
commands.description: Save explicit durable user memory: stable preferences, facts, or standing instructions that should affect future phone behavior. Do not use for one-off reminders or future conditions; use commitments or watchers for those.

commands.name: openphone.watchers.list
commands.description: List active OpenPhone watchers: external-condition monitors that may alert later. Use when the user asks what OpenPhone is watching or what conditions are being monitored. Do not use for queued background agent jobs; use background_job_list for those.

limit.description: Maximum number of watchers to return.

commands.name: openphone.watchers.create
commands.description: Create a durable OpenPhone watcher: a future trigger plus an optional reaction. Use for proactive monitoring of message replies/no-reply deadlines, calls/no-call deadlines, notification matches, web/page changes, semantic page conditions, and requests like tell me when, remind me if, watch this, let me know, or make sure I do not forget. If the user asks the phone to do something when the trigger happens, store that reaction in delivery using delivery.tool plus delivery.arguments, or delivery.prompt for a background agent job. Tool arguments may reference trigger data with templates such as {{event.number}}, {{event.address}}, {{event.title}}, {{event.text}}, {{event.url}}, {{watcher.id}}, and {{watcher.title}}. For requests to monitor incoming SMS/messages and check, research, classify, summarize, or otherwise reason about each message, create a message watcher with match_any=true and recurring=true, then use delivery.prompt so the fired watcher starts a background agent job; the prompt should include {{event.text}} and may tell the agent to use browser_search or browser_fetch_page for internet context before notifying the user. Do not use watcher_create for explicit run-as-soon-as-possible queued agent work; use background_job_create for that.

source.description: Generic source such as web, notification, message, call, time, or screen.

query.description: Condition text for text or semantic evaluators.

url.description: Web URL when source is web.

type.description: Compatibility type: time, notification, web_change, message_reply, or call_back.

address.description: Phone number or sender to watch when source is message.

number.description: Phone number to watch when source is call. Omit and set match_any=true for every caller.

match_any.description: For call watchers, true means match any phone number. Use for requests like every incoming call or any caller.

direction.description: For call watchers: any (default), incoming, or outgoing.

thread_id.description: SMS thread id to watch when source is message.

deadline_at.description: Unix milliseconds deadline for message or call watchers; with notify_on no_reply or no_call, fires if nothing arrived by then.

notify_on.description: For message watchers: reply (default) or no_reply. For call watchers: call (default; alert when a call with the number appears) or no_call (alert only if the deadline passes without one).

delivery.description: Delivery or reaction details. Use {"mode":"notification"} for alerts, {"tool":"model_tool_name","arguments":{...}} for a stored tool reaction, or {"mode":"background_job","prompt":"..."} for an observe-first background agent turn. For SMS research/classification/evaluation watchers, use delivery.prompt with {{event.text}} and browser_search/browser_fetch_page instructions rather than hardcoding the check in the watcher. Set notify=false or mode=silent to avoid a notification after the reaction.

tool.description: Shortcut for delivery.tool: the model-facing tool to run when the watcher fires.

arguments.description: Shortcut for delivery.arguments. Values may use event templates such as {{event.number}}.

prompt.description: Shortcut for delivery.prompt when the watcher should enqueue a background agent job after the trigger.

recurring.description: True when the watcher should continue after matching, such as every matching call or notification.

sms_body.description: Legacy shortcut only; prefer delivery.tool with delivery.arguments for new watcher reactions.

interval_ms.description: Polling interval in milliseconds if relevant.

commands.name: openphone.watchers.stop
commands.description: Stop active OpenPhone watchers. Use watcher_id when the user names a specific watcher id. Use scope=all or all=true when the user asks to turn off, disable, clear, remove, delete, cancel, stop, or kill all watchers. Use query when the user describes a watcher by title, source, or condition instead of giving an id.

watcher_id.description: Specific watcher id. Omit when stopping all watchers or a query-matched set.

scope.description: Use all to stop every active watcher.

all.description: True to stop every active watcher.

query.description: Optional search text to stop matching active watchers when the user described the watcher but did not provide an id.

commands.name: openphone.jobs.list
commands.description: List durable OpenPhone background agent jobs, including active, running, completed, failed, and stopped jobs. Use when the user asks what OpenPhone is working on in the background or what background jobs are active.

limit.description: Maximum number of jobs to return.

reason.description: Why background job context is needed.

commands.name: openphone.jobs.create
commands.description: Create a durable OpenPhone background agent job: a queued agent turn that can run after the current conversation. Use for explicit requests like create a background job, do this in the background, run this as soon as possible, keep working and notify me, periodically summarize, check later by running an agent task, or run a non-urgent retrieval task. Do not use watcher_create for these queued agent jobs. Background jobs may observe and summarize; state-changing tools require foreground review.

prompt.description: The agent turn to run when the job wakes.

type.description: agent_turn, system_event, or heartbeat. Default agent_turn.

schedule.description: Schedule fields such as next_run_at or interval_ms.

run_at.description: Unix milliseconds when the job should first run. Omit or 0 to run soon.

next_run_at.description: Unix milliseconds when the job should next run.

delivery.description: Delivery mode. Use {"mode":"notification"}, {"mode":"silent"}, or {"mode":"none"}. If the user gives exact notification wording, include it as notification_text, text, or message.

notification_text.description: Optional exact notification body to show when the background job completes. Use this when the user says to notify them with an exact phrase.

payload.description: Optional structured payload for future runtime integrations.

reason.description: Why this background job should exist.

commands.name: openphone.jobs.stop
commands.description: Stop a durable OpenPhone background agent job.

reason.description: Why this background job should stop.

commands.name: openphone.app.open
commands.description: Open an installed Android app. This tool completes only simple goals whose whole intent is to open, launch, or show the app. If the user wants something done inside the app, such as playing media, searching, selecting content, changing settings, ordering, booking, posting, sending, or any other in-app workflow, use the autonomous multi-step agent: open the app as one step, then keep observing and acting until the requested result is visibly complete or blocked.

label.description: Optional explicit app label when package_or_label is a package name.

commands.name: openphone.url.open
commands.description: Open a URL directly in the browser.

commands.description: Tap absolute screen coordinates.

commands.name: openphone.ui.tap_element

reason.description: Why this element should be tapped.

commands.name: openphone.ui.long_press

commands.name: openphone.ui.long_press_element
commands.description: Long press an enabled interactive element by screen element id.

reason.description: Why this element should be long-pressed.

commands.name: openphone.ui.swipe
commands.description: Swipe from one coordinate to another.

commands.name: openphone.ui.type_text
commands.description: Type text into the focused field.

commands.name: openphone.input.press_key
commands.description: Press a supported Android navigation or IME key.

key.description: back, home, recents, enter, search, or go.

commands.name: openphone.clipboard.set

commands.name: openphone.clipboard.paste
